page: Amazon Web Services r7g.large is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and custom 64-bit ARM architecture. It features 2 dedicated physical cores on the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor running at 2.6 GHz, with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Cache resources include 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and a shared 32 MB L3 cache. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core tests, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, register average performance tiers, supported by single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core and multi-threaded benchmarks, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, fall into the weak category due to the low absolute core count of two. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance for large-block uncached reads but drop to weak for small-block cached reads and large-block writes.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio, the r7g.large is designed for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallel compute power. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for applications needing a larger memory footprint relative to compute resources, avoiding the need to provision larger, more expensive instances just to satisfy memory requirements. Suitable workloads include caching layers, lightweight database operations, text processing, and memory-bound general-purpose applications. It is less suited for heavy multi-threaded processing, compilation, or cryptographic tasks, where its benchmark scores are poor or weak.
description: The Amazon Web Services r7g.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated physical cores on the 64-bit ARM-based AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor running at 2.6 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 16.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU acceleration, relying on a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data reveals average single-core CPU and large-block memory read performance, contrasted by weak multi-core and write bandwidth results due to its dual-core configuration. This profile makes the r7g.large qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive but compute-light workloads, such as caching, text processing, and lightweight database operations, while remaining less suitable for highly parallelized tasks or intensive compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7g.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 ARM-based Graviton Neoverse-V1 cores, 16.0 GB of RAM, and a 1 Gbps baseline network, designed for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7g.large is a memory-optimized instance with 2 ARM-based Graviton cores, 16.0 GB RAM, and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A memory-optimized ARM instance delivering balanced single-core performance and high memory density for lightweight, memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 2 dedicated ARM-based AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 cores at 2.6 GHz.
Equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core.
Features a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and no local storage.
Delivers average single-core CPU and large-block memory read performance.
Best suited for caching layers, text processing, and lightweight database operations.
